title: Iberis dunalii
intro: Iberis dunalii, also known as Dwarf Candytuft or Candytuft, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and fragrance. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Iberis dunalii include sesquiterpenoids and phenolic compounds, such as isothiocyanates and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Iberis dunalii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to soothe skin conditions and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Iberis dunalii is classified within the family Brassicaceae, also known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family comprises a wide range of flowering plants, often characterized by their four-petaled flowers and cruciform seed pods. The genus Iberis is a group of flowering plants native to Europe and North Africa.
